behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model output quality from a real speech-to-text/diarization API: mocks can preserve labels by construction and hide real label drop/misattribution (MOCK-HIDE, LLM-DEP via real AI/ML call). Not Workflow: behavior text targets one model stage (STT label preservation), not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT provider with speaker diarization enabled, then verify emitted transcript segments retain speaker IDs/names consistently enough for downstream attendee attribution input mapping.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is parser/transform logic on transcript text and schema mapping within a local function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS says this specific failure is algorithmic, not inherently cross-module). Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no requirement for real network/OS/model output, only extraction correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets into the task-assignment parser and assert exact structured outputs for assignee and task text, including edge cases like quoted speech and multiple assignments in one utterance.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real summarizer model behavior (coverage/omission of action items per assignee), which deterministic stubs can mask (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow: can be validated from a fixed transcript through one live summarization step; multiple agentic stages are not required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use a transcript fixture with known attendee-task assignments, call the real summarization model in production prompt/config mode, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one corresponding action item in structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic routing logic once decisions are already identified; failure is local mapping/schema placement, not real infra or model judgment (MOCK-MISS). Higher levels are disqualified by behavior wording: it tests routing of identified decisions, not whether a model finds them.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a normalized intermediate object containing identified decisions into the routing function and assert outputs populate `decisions` schema fields and do not leak into narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This tests model decision selection/placement quality in live summarization; mocked responses can always place decisions correctly and hide real misses (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Not Workflow: behavior can be exercised with provided transcript to single live summarization call; multi-step agent chain is not required by text.
behaviors.test_description: Submit transcripts with known explicit decisions to the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section while narrative text does not contain unmatched decision-only items.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequenced processing across segments plus merge coherence, which is multiple agentic steps in order (MULTI-STEP). Integration is disqualified because one model decision per segment and merge quality can fail only with real model outputs; pure stubs can hide incoherent cross-segment synthesis.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production segmentation settings, execute sequential transcription/summarization over all chunks, then verify final merged output is a single structured summary that preserves cross-segment action items and decisions without duplication or contradiction.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real STT + real LLM steps and handoffs; failures arise in chained agentic stages and orchestration (MULTI-STEP, REAL-INFRA, LLM-DEP). Lower levels are disqualified by explicit 'full pipeline' and 'real meeting recording' wording.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like run from real audio upload through transcription, summarization, and structured delivery endpoint, asserting successful completion, valid schema output, and no human retries/intervention.
